title: Cheilocostus lacerus
taxonomy: Cheilocostus lacerus is a species of flowering plant in the family Costaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It is a member of the Costaceae family, which consists of approximately 15 species of plants. The family Costaceae is part of the order Zingiberales, which includes many other types of plants such as ginger and cardamom. Cheilocostus lacerus is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant that belongs to the genus Cheilocostus. The classification of Cheilocostus lacerus is further divided into various ranks, including kingdom, class, order, family, genus, and species.
uses: Cheilocostus lacerus is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have shown potential in treating various health conditions. The plant is traditionally used to manage fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Cheilocostus lacerus has been studied for its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.

These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and potential therapeutic applications.
